tasks.text: Fix the pre-existing Cloud Terminal prod backend crash loop caused by a deleted Codex binary path (`ENOENT` under `@openai/.codex-*`) so the proxy no longer continuously restarts the prod target during Hermes validation.
tasks.moreWork: I could not find the stale `@openai/.codex-*` launch path or the prod-target restart logic inside this Hermes repo. The evidence points to an external Cloud Terminal runtime/config issue outside `/home/ubuntu/cloud-terminal-data/projects/hermes-webui` that needs reproduction details or a coordinated fix in the sibling Cloud Terminal backend/runtime code.

tasks.text: Port the remaining global Cloud Terminal menu destinations into Hermes `/ops` so the home action strip can expose true equivalents for deployments, files, todos, and terminal/back navigation instead of the current reduced menu shell.

Continue porting the remaining Hermes-owned `/ops` side panels onto Cloud Terminal shell patterns so run artifacts/logs/events and the deeper deployment/database/health/play detail panels no longer mix Cloud Terminal cards with legacy Hermes panel styling.

Stop live `/api/ops/sessions` from surfacing stale test-generated and orphaned task sessions in the real Hermes dashboard so the Ops activity/project views do not fill with unnamed or irrelevant historical entries from old pytest/runtime artifacts.
When I am in the epics page and try to write a task text for a new task, it is wiped clean every 5 seconds so its almost impossible to add a new task. Fix this.
When I click the session under the active sessions in the ops dashboard I should be taken to the session, right now nothing happens.
When I click complete on a task in the epics page it says "Internal server error"

When on mobile and pressing the back button it doesn't go back properly, if I am in the ops dashboard or in a session or in the project page it always throws me back to the landing page for Hermes web ui
I seem to only be getting done notifications and not any play notifications. I want you to analyse the project in depth and make sure that we have play notifications correctly set up.
The quick task runner UI lacks the toggle for setting the task as a goal, look at how it is done in cloud terminal

I want to add a toggle to the quick task runner that when toggled, sets the task as a goal by using the /goal command in hermes agent. I want you to look online to see exactly how it works so your implementation is correct.
There is a bug in the ops dashboard, where if I remove all notifications, I dismiss them, then I go back and in again, they're still there, so they're not truly dismissed.
I created a task and executed it, bit it didnt show up in the actove sessions list in the ops dashboard. Instead insee 15 unnamed tasks?

Analyze the current project task file and execute the ready tasks with AI.
Follow the project task file as the source of truth for execution order and status updates.
Branch: master
Tasks JSON file for this branch: /home/ubuntu/cloud-terminal-data/projects/hermes-webui/project_tasks/master.json
Read and update that JSON file directly for this branch.
Process tasks sequentially with no user prompts.
Actionable tasks are only:
- ready: not done, not blocked by dependencies, no qaStatus, and not inProgress.
- needs-more-work: qaStatus is "needs-more-work".
Execution loop for each actionable task:
1) Reload task JSON and choose the next actionable task.
2) Implement the task in the codebase.
3) Immediately update the task JSON before moving on.
If you discover new follow-up work while executing, add it as a new ready task with done=false, no qaStatus, and no inProgress.
Never set qaStatus="ready-for-test" on newly created follow-up tasks; that status is only for actionable tasks you just executed.
4) If task is completed, keep done=false, set qaStatus="ready-for-test", and clear moreWork/inProgress/sessionId/lastSessionAt when present.
5) If task cannot be completed now, keep done=false, set qaStatus="needs-more-work", and write a clear moreWork note.
6) Save the JSON and continue to the next actionable task until none remain.
